pr.title: stop mpColl_80046904 from inlining its helpers
pr.author: BR-

summary: Added a local CodeWarrior pragma region around mpColl_80046904 in src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c to prevent helper functions inside or associated with that function from being inlined. The PR was a very small matching-control change: three added lines, no deletions, no discussion, and no review comments.
what_changed: Inserted `#pragma push` immediately before `#pragma dont_inline on` near the start of `mpColl_80046904`.
Inserted `#pragma dont_inline on` immediately before the definition of `bool mpColl_80046904(CollData* coll, u32 flags)`.
Inserted `#pragma pop` immediately after the end of `mpColl_80046904`, before `mpColl_8004730C`.
The change scopes the no-inline behavior to `mpColl_80046904` rather than affecting later code in `mpcoll.c`.
smart_moves: Used `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` to keep the `dont_inline` setting tightly scoped around the problematic function.
Avoided changing logic in a collision routine; the diff only adjusts compiler behavior, reducing risk of behavioral drift.
Placed the pragma region around the full `mpColl_80046904` body, which is consistent with the PR title's goal of stopping helper inlining for that function.
decomp_lessons: For functions whose helpers are incorrectly inlined by the compiler, a scoped `#pragma dont_inline on` can be a minimal matching tactic.
When using compiler pragmas for matching, wrap them with `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` so the setting does not leak into following functions.
A tiny pragma-only change can be appropriate when the source logic is already correct but compiler inlining decisions prevent a match.
Collision code matching should prefer compiler-control fixes over rewriting stable logic when the mismatch is due to inlining.
naming_conventions: `mpColl_80046904` follows the project convention of retaining address-based names for not-yet-semantic collision functions.
`mpColl_8004730C` and `mpColl_80043C6C` show the same `mpColl_` plus address naming pattern in `mpcoll.c`.
Local collision flag macros use descriptive names such as `CollisionFlagAir_PlatformPassCallback` and `CollisionFlagAir_CanGrabLedge`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use CodeWarrior pragmas `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` to control inlining locally.
Place `dont_inline` immediately before the function whose generated code is affected.
Restore pragma state immediately after the function to avoid changing code generation for subsequent functions.
This PR provides evidence for an inlining-related match issue, but the dump does not include the exact helper functions or before/after assembly differences.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that add `#pragma dont_inline on` for matching.
Search for scoped `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` usage in `src/melee/mp`.
Inspect later work on `mpColl_80046904` for semantic renaming or helper extraction.
Compare other collision functions where helper inlining affected matching.
